name: Baked Salmon Croquettes
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds fresh salmon, cooked and flaked (about 3 cups)
2 cups bread crumbs made from 2 slices of quality white bread with crusts removed
1 tablespoon scallion, white part only, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Thoroughly grease 6 individual ramekins or custard cups with 1 tablespoon of butter.
3. In a bowl, combine the flaked salmon, 3/4 cup bread crumbs, scallion, dill, lemon zest, egg, and heavy cream. Mix gently with a fork.
4. Season the mixture with salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper.
5. Divide the mixture among the buttered cups and pack it down slightly.
6. Top each croquette with the remaining 1/4 cup of bread crumbs and dot with the remaining tablespoon of butter.
7. Arrange the cups in a roasting pan. Pour enough hot water into the pan to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
8. Bake until firm and set, about 30 minutes.
9. Allow the croquettes to cool for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
10. Serve each croquette topped with sour cream and caviar, or simply garnish with lemon wedges.
